Title: The Vacuolar Inositol Transporter BvINT1;1 Contributes to Raffinose Biosynthesis and Reactive Oxygen Species Scavenging During Cold Stress in Sugar Beet

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Bv3_054860_wjth.t2; BVRB_3F054860; mutant SNP RefBeet-1.2: Bvchr3.sca003:c4242591
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest expressed INT homolog; strongly induced by low temperature, especially in taproot (up to ~9-fold at 4°C); lower induction in leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging/quenching during cold stress; contributes to freezing/cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cytosolic inositol availability from the vacuole, thereby supporting cold-induced galactinol/raffinose accumulation and ROS homeostasis; loss of function causes inositol accumulation, reduced raffinose in taproot under cold, elevated ROS-marker expression, reduced biomass and impaired photosynthetic performance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling from RNA-seq and RT-qPCR; subcellular localization in Arabidopsis protoplasts; dileucine motif mis-targeting assay; Xenopus oocyte transport electrophysiology; EMS loss-of-function mutant analysis; vacuole metabolite analysis; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (for transient localization constructs in heterologous assays)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvINT1;1 is the only sugar beet INT homolog strongly induced by cold, especially in taproots. 2. BvINT1;1 localizes to the tonoplast and functions as a highly specific myo-inositol/H+ symporter exporting inositol from the vacuole to the cytosol. 3. Loss of BvINT1;1 traps inositol in vacuoles, lowers cold-induced raffinose accumulation in taproots, and triggers stronger ROS-related responses under cold. 4. BvINT1;1 deficiency reduces root biomass and impairs photosynthetic performance during cold acclimation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a transporter, not a pathway enzyme or TF regulator. A conserved C-terminal dileucine motif (LL489/490) is required for tonoplast targeting; LL489/490AA relocalizes protein to plasma membrane for transport assays. Premature stop mutant W188STOP predicted nonfunctional with only five TMDs instead of 12.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces root biomass even under control conditions and further impairs shoot/root biomass and photosystem II performance under cold
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering improved cold tolerance in sugar beet by enhancing vacuolar inositol export and downstream raffinose-mediated ROS protection; caution that transporter localization/function must be preserved, including the C-terminal dileucine motif

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in shoots and taproots but not clearly induced by low temperature
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization in Arabidopsis protoplasts; comparative expression analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvINT1;2 is a close homolog of AtINT1 and localizes to the tonoplast. 2. Unlike BvINT1;1, BvINT1;2 is not strongly cold induced. 3. It is discussed as a possible partially compensating isoform, but no direct metabolite phenotype was demonstrated in this paper.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core/background-borderline gene because it was experimentally localized in Results, but the primary causal metabolite evidence centers on BvINT1;1; no direct functional metabolite validation for BvINT1;2 in this study.
